mappings.rationaleAI: The Iron Age covers the technology of iron smelting for tools and weapons replacing bronze, directly relevant to the early history of hand tools and the development of metal tools.

The Industrial Revolution covers innovations in textile manufacturing machinery (spinning jenny, water frame, power loom) and the factory system, relevant to the history and characteristics of machine tools.
The Antikythera Mechanism's complex gear system for astronomical calculation demonstrates ancient Greek mastery of machine mechanisms and gears.
Neanderthal Mousterian tool technology represents evidence for early stone tool making, relevant to the Paleolithic development of hand tools.
Homo erectus's Acheulean hand axes represent a key stage in the early history of stone hand tools.
Automata demonstrate machine mechanisms from ancient pneumatic devices through medieval clockwork to connections with robotics, relevant to mechanical devices and computer-aided machinery.
The Stephensons' engineering work including locomotive development and bridge design involved advances in machine tool technology during the Industrial Revolution.
Ada Lovelace's work on Babbage's Analytical Engine and the first computer algorithm connects to the origins of computer-aided design and manufacturing.
